description:
You are the on–call system administrator and have been asked to carry out two
    quick GPG-related maintenance actions on the server.

    Action 1 – Encrypt the credentials file
    ---------------------------------------
      • Source file (already present): /home/user/secrets/db_credentials.txt  
      • Use *symmetric* GPG encryption (that is, “-c” / “--symmetric”).  
      • Mandatory pass-phrase:  orbitals  
      • Output file: /home/user/secrets/db_credentials.txt.gpg  

      After the operation is finished the original plaintext file
      /home/user/secrets/db_credentials.txt must stay untouched and readable,
      while a **non-empty** binary file named
      /home/user/secrets/db_credentials.txt.gpg has to exist in the same
      directory.

    Action 2 – Verify the vendor patch
    ----------------------------------
      • Data file (already present):
            /home/user/incoming/patch_v1.tar.gz
      • Detached ASCII signature (already present):
            /home/user/incoming/patch_v1.tar.gz.asc

      Use GPG to verify the detached signature (i.e. “gpg --verify …”).
      The result of the verification has to be written to the **one-line**
      log file  
            /home/user/logs/patch_v1_verification.log

      The log file **must contain exactly one of the two strings below and
      nothing else** (no leading/trailing spaces, no blank lines):

          VERIFICATION: SUCCESS
          VERIFICATION: FAILED

      – Write “VERIFICATION: SUCCESS” if GPG’s verification command exits with
        return code 0 (good signature).  
      – Otherwise write “VERIFICATION: FAILED”.

    The automated grader will check the following:

      1.  /home/user/secrets/db_credentials.txt.gpg exists,
          is non-empty and its size differs from the plaintext file.  
      2.  /home/user/secrets/db_credentials.txt still exists unchanged.  
      3.  /home/user/logs/patch_v1_verification.log exists and contains
          *exactly* “VERIFICATION: FAILED” or “VERIFICATION: SUCCESS” in the
          format specified above (single line, case-sensitive).  

    Only standard command-line GPG tools available in a typical Linux
    environment may be used; no GUI interaction is expected.

    NOTE: Do **not** delete or move any of the pre-existing files or
    directories while completing the task.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing directory structure and file contents that the
        automated tests and the agent will start with.
    -->

    1. Directory: /home/user/secrets          (mode 700, owner=user)
       └─ File: /home/user/secrets/db_credentials.txt  (mode 600)
          Exact contents (two lines, trailing newline after each line):
              db_user=admin
              db_password=Sup3rS3cr3t

    2. Directory: /home/user/incoming         (mode 755, owner=user)
       ├─ File: /home/user/incoming/patch_v1.tar.gz      (mode 644)
       │   Contents (plain text, **NOT** a real tarball):
       │       dummy patch content
       │
       └─ File: /home/user/incoming/patch_v1.tar.gz.asc  (mode 644)
           Contents (intentionally mismatching, causes bad signature):
               -----BEGIN PGP SIGNATURE-----
               Version: GnuPG v1

               dGVzdCBzaWduYXR1cmUK
               =abcd
               -----END PGP SIGNATURE-----

    3. Directory: /home/user/logs            (mode 755, owner=user)
       – starts empty.  The agent must create
         /home/user/logs/patch_v1_verification.log

    Expected end state for grading:
      • /home/user/secrets/db_credentials.txt  unchanged.
      • /home/user/secrets/db_credentials.txt.gpg  exists and non-empty.
      • /home/user/logs/patch_v1_verification.log
          – single line reading exactly “VERIFICATION: FAILED”
            (because the signature file does not match the data).
    -->
name: task_000000_b314bfef
